title: Hypovolemic Hyponatremia
buzzwords: low serum sodium with volume depletion
orthostatic hypotension and poor skin turgor
history of vomiting, diarrhea, or sweating
low effective circulating volume triggers ADH
urine sodium <25 mEq/L with high urine osmolality
RAAS activation drives water retention
treat with isotonic saline to restore volume
loop diuretic or thiazide-induced GI losses

title: Hypervolemic Hyponatremia
buzzwords: edematous patient with low serum sodium
peripheral edema and jugular venous distention
ascites with proteinuria or heart failure
increased total body sodium and proportionally more water
low effective circulating volume despite total overload
urine sodium <25 mEq/L from RAAS activation
seen in CHF, cirrhosis, nephrotic syndrome
treat underlying cause plus loop diuretics

title: SIADH (Euvolemic Hyponatremia)
buzzwords: euvolemic patient with low serum sodium
low BUN and low serum uric acid
inappropriately concentrated urine despite dilute serum
urine osmolality >100 mOsm/kg with hypotonic plasma
urine sodium >40 mEq/L while euvolemic
SSRIs, carbamazepine, MDMA, or cyclophosphamide
treat with water restriction and vaptans
small cell lung cancer with ectopic ADH

title: Primary (Psychogenic) Polydipsia
buzzwords: euvolemic hyponatremia from excess water intake
compulsive drinking in a psychiatric patient
intake exceeds kidneys' free-water excretion capacity
marathon runner drinking only pure water on cool day
appropriately dilute urine with low urine sodium
urine osmolality low, distinguishing from SIADH
schizophrenia patient with chronic water drinking
treat with strict fluid restriction

title: Pseudohyponatremia (Isotonic Hyponatremia)
buzzwords: low measured sodium with normal serum osmolality
lab artifact, not a true sodium deficit
expanded non-aqueous plasma phase
severe hyperlipidemia or hypertriglyceridemia
hyperproteinemia from monoclonal gammopathy
multiple myeloma with paraprotein elevation
no treatment needed beyond fixing underlying cause
flame photometry artifact from lipids/proteins

title: Hypertonic Hyponatremia
buzzwords: low serum sodium with high serum osmolality
osmotic shift of water out of cells
another solute is driving plasma tonicity
sodium drops ~1.6 mEq/L per 100 mg/dL glucose
neurosurgical patient on mannitol infusion
diabetic ketoacidosis with severe hyperglycemia
hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state in type 2 diabetic
treat by lowering glucose with insulin

title: Osmotic Demyelination Syndrome
buzzwords: neurologic complication of overzealous correction
delayed dysarthria, dysphagia, and quadriparesis
locked-in syndrome days after sodium correction
iatrogenic demyelination from rapid osmolar shift
sodium raised more than 8-10 mEq/L in 24 hours
chronic hyponatremia corrected too quickly
central pontine myelinolysis on MRI
prevent by limiting rise to 4-6 mEq/L per day
